Title: Wilkinson, Charles
Subject Area: ancient|Early Western World|Egyptian (ancient)|Mediterranean (Early Western World)|Near Eastern (Early Western World)
Overview: Founder of the Department of Ancient Near Eastern Art at the Metropolitan Museum of Art, New York. Wilkinson began as an assistant (copyist) to Albert M. Lythgoe in his Egyptian excavations for the Metropolitan Museum of Art.
Sources: Tomkins, Calvin. Merchants and Masterpieces: The Story of the Metropolitan Museum of Art. 2nd. ed. New York: Henry Holt, 1989, p. 137.
